description_long: This machine gun features a robust cylindrical metal barrel with a perforated heat shield, likely for improved cooling during sustained fire. The receiver and barrel assembly are finished in a matte black, contrasting with the wooden stock and foregrip, which have a smooth, ergonomic design and visible fasteners. The stock is fixed and made of wood, providing stability and a classic appearance. The weapon includes a simple iron sight system, with a front post and a rear notch, both integrated into the top of the barrel and receiver. There is no visible magazine or ammunition feed system attached, and the underside of the receiver features a vertical grip or mounting point. The overall construction suggests a blend of metal and wood, typical of mid-20th-century submachine guns.
description: A compact machine gun with a black perforated barrel, wooden stock and foregrip, and simple iron sights. The design combines metal and wood for a classic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the machine gun, highlighting the perforated barrel, wooden stock, and foregrip.
description_view_1: Rear view focusing on the end of the wooden stock and the alignment of the barrel, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the barrel, stock, and foregrip from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view down the barrel, emphasizing the circular muzzle and the perforated heat shield, with the stock barely visible.
attribute_barrel_type: standard cylindrical barrel with perforated heat shield
attribute_stock_design: fixed wooden stock with ergonomic contour
attribute_iron_sight_configuration: simple notch rear sight with fixed front post
